fields.description:
## What
One-time backfill of `close_class` for already-closed bugs that lack it, inferred from STRUCTURAL signals only (no prose recovery, no LLM guessing): a bug whose reduced `reverts` contains a revert of a STATUS/COMMITS event â†’ `regression`; a bug with a plan-review `BLOCK` in its `REVIEW_RESULT` history â†’ `plan_defect`; a bug with a `gate_error_v1` ERROR on its sidecar streams â†’ `env_integration`; otherwise â†’ `undetermined`. Every backfilled value is labeled and segregated from authoritative going-forward `--class` values.

## Data-access ground truth (the plan is built on this)
- **Only backfill bugs where `state["close_class"]` is ABSENT** (a bug already closed with `--class` keeps its authoritative value; `close_class` is folded on the `*â†’closed` edge, `_processors.py:195-209`). Reduce via `reduce_ticket`.
- **REVERT signal**: reduced `state["reverts"]` is a list of `{target_event_uuid, target_event_type, timestamp, ...}` (`_processors.py:452-462`) â€” a revert of a SPECIFIC prior event of ANY type. A "regression" inference requires a revert whose `target_event_type` is `STATUS` or `COMMITS` (a substantive revert), not a revert of a COMMENT/LINK.
- **plan-review BLOCK signal**: `REVIEW_RESULT` events are reducer-ignored (`_version.py:77`) and NOT in compiled state â€” read them via `rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.all_review_results(id, repo_root=)` (returns retained payloads; check `verdict=="BLOCK"`). (Retention caps at 50/ticket, so a very old BLOCK may be pruned â€” an accepted best-effort limit, logged.)
- **env_integration signal**: a `gate_error_v1` ERROR record on the bug's `REVIEW_RESULT`/`COMPLETION_VERDICT` streams (raw scan; schema `gate_error_v1`).
- These signals are **heuristic evidence, not proof**: the structural facts (a REVERT of a STATUS event; a BLOCK verdict; an ERROR record) are reliable, but the class they imply is an inference. Anything not confidently inferable â†’ `undetermined` (the explicit escape bucket in `CLOSE_CLASSES`).

## Files
- `scripts/backfill_bug_class.py` â€” new standalone script: iterate closed bugs lacking `close_class`, classify from the signals above, and write a labeled backfill record (NOT a STATUS event â€” a separate backfill artifact) carrying `source="backfill_classified"`, `confidence="classified"`, the inferred `close_class`, and the evidence (which signal fired). Idempotent (re-running does not duplicate). `--dry-run` prints without writing.
- `tests/scripts/test_backfill_bug_class.py` â€” new (tier `tests/scripts/`, `pytestmark = pytest.mark.scripts` â€” NOT the nonexistent `tests/unit/scripts/`).

## Approach
The original free-text reasons were discarded, so only structural signals are trustworthy. Classifying solely from REVERT / plan-review BLOCK / gate ERROR linkage (and `undetermined` for everything else) keeps the backfill honest and avoids contamination. Every backfilled record carries `source=backfill_classified, confidence=classified` so the c085 registry keeps them apart from authoritative structural series (`is_authoritative("backfill_classified")` is False).

## Tests
- Unit (`tests/scripts/`, real temp store; craft closed bugs with the signals): a bug with a REVERT of a STATUS event â†’ `regression`; a bug with a `REVIEW_RESULT` BLOCK â†’ `plan_defect`; a bug with a `gate_error_v1` ERROR â†’ `env_integration`; a plain closed bug with none â†’ `undetermined`; a bug already carrying `close_class` is SKIPPED (not re-backfilled). Every emitted record carries `source=backfill_classified` + `confidence=classified`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/scripts/test_backfill_bug_class.py` passes (tier `scripts`): the four fixtures classify to `regression`, `plan_defect`, `env_integration`, `undetermined` respectively (exact-value asserts on the emitted record's `close_class`).
- [ ] a bug that already has `state["close_class"]` is NOT overwritten/re-emitted (idempotence + authoritative-value protection) â€” asserted.
- [ ] every backfilled record carries `source=="backfill_classified"` and `confidence=="classified"`, and `is_authoritative(record.source)` is False â€” asserted on the output, proving segregation from authoritative series.
- [ ] a REVERT whose only `target_event_type` is `COMMENT` does NOT yield `regression` (it falls through to `undetermined`) â€” asserted, proving the substantive-revert filter.
